sprint: Magic Card Identity Evolution -- Phase 2: Cloud Identity, Recall, and Two-Sided Card Art
task: Closes the two gaps Phase 1's own header disclosed: the identity had zero cloud presence, and the ceremony's card had no real card-ness. Confirmed via AskUserQuestion: store the raw pattern on Supabase, safeguarded via RLS lockdown only (mirroring cards/redeem_card()'s zero-SELECT-exposure precedent, never a leaky direct SELECT); build the full tap-to-recall screen in this same pass, not just the storage foundation. Architecture: local stays authoritative everywhere, cloud is a continuous fire-and-forget background mirror once a card is claimed -- a Visitor never triggers any network call at all. New supabase/schema.sql tables magic_card_identities (client-supplied text id, matching js/magicCard.js's own local mc_... id -- a real mid-implementation design correction from the plan's original server-generated uuid, avoiding an unnecessary capture-and-persist round trip) and magic_card_recalls (no expires_at -- permanent recognition, not a time-boxed unlock), plus a new SECURITY DEFINER recall_magic_card() RPC (a near-mirror of redeem_card(), reusing _card_platform_sort_pattern() verbatim, verified via static leak-proof check to never echo the pattern back on any branch) and a new creator_projects table (Creator's first-ever cloud project backup, mirroring builder_projects exactly) with an additive cross-owner SELECT-only policy keyed off a live magic_card_recalls row. New js/creatorProjectSync.js mirrors World Builder's own projectSync.js near-verbatim. js/magicCard.js extended with claim()/touch()/rename() cloud-sync hooks and new recall()/adopt() functions -- adopt() threads the client-already-known pattern (the RPC never returns it) into a full local card record and pulls the recalled owner's projects as brand-new local copies with freshly minted ids, never the original. New recall UI in js/creationFlow.js reuses the existing tap-grid mechanic verbatim, reachable from Screen 1 and the Shared Device picker. New js/magicCardArt.js renders a genuine two-sided card (front: nickname/claim-date; back: the real constellation plotted from the card's own pattern) at print resolution with Download/Print, wired into both the Awakening ceremony's First Claimed Moment and a new View My Card action on Magic Card Home. Verified end-to-end via a new 23-assertion Playwright suite against a mocked Supabase client (this sandbox cannot reach a real Supabase project): real Publish-to-claim ceremony, correct cloud push payload/timing, two real distinct card-art canvases, real browser downloads, continuous project sync gated correctly on claimed-card-only, and a full simulated-new-device recall producing the correct adopted identity and a freshly-id'd adopted project. docs/VIHU_CARD_PLATFORM.md gained a clarifying section distinguishing Magic Card identities from the separate, reserved-but-unused card_type='creator' Vihu Card slot. Full regression across the existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright suite (including both Phase 1 suites) and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged, zero console errors. Live end-to-end verification against a real Supabase project remains deferred to the user's own browser.
